info.title: MangaBaka Library API & Limits
info.description: Subset of the MangaBaka API focused on library management and system limits.

get.description: The OAuth token must have the `library.read`

schema.description: Filter by publication start date >= this value. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-D.D

parameters.description: Filter by publication start date >= this value. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-D.D
parameters.name: published_start_date_lower

schema.description: Filter by publication start date <= this value. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.description: Filter by publication start date <= this value. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD
parameters.name: published_start_date_upper

schema.description: Filter by publication end date >= this value. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.description: Filter by publication end date >= this value. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD
parameters.name: published_end_date_lower

schema.description: Filter by publication end date <= this value. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.description: Filter by publication end date <= this value. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD
parameters.name: published_end_date_upper

schema.description: Any licensed state is used when omitted. When "true" only licensed series are returned. When "false" only unlicensed series are returned

parameters.description: Any licensed state is used when omitted. When "true" only licensed series are returned. When "false" only unlicensed series are returned
parameters.name: is_licensed

schema.description: This a alpha feature, it may be removed or changed without any warning

parameters.description: This a alpha feature, it may be removed or changed without any warning
parameters.name: exclude_user_library

schema.description: Can optionally be used with [sort_by=random] to ensure consistent sorting, allowing users to go back to "random" results or share them with others.

parameters.description: Can optionally be used with [sort_by=random] to ensure consistent sorting, allowing users to go back to "random" results or share them with others.
parameters.name: random_seed

limit.description: The number of rows per page

next.description: URL for the next page. If `null` there are no next page

previous.description: URL for the previous page. If `null` there are no previous page

get.description: The OAuth token must have the `library.read` or `library.write` scope

post.description: The OAuth token must have the `library.write` scope

put.description:
The OAuth token must have the `library.write` scope.
You can omit fields you do not want to change - e.g. sending just `{"rating": 2}` will succeed

patch.summary: Update a library entry (with partial data)
patch.description:
The OAuth token must have the `library.write` scope.
You can omit fields you do not want to change - e.g. sending just `{"rating": 2}` will succeed.
This is CURRENTLY identical to PUT, since all fields are optional, but that might change in the future.

delete.description: The OAuth token must have the `library.write` scope

get.summary: Get a list of library entries
get.description: The OAuth token must have the `library.read` scope

schema.description: List of series (up to 50) to look up in the users library. If a series is not in the users library, it will silently be omitted from the response

parameters.description: List of series (up to 50) to look up in the users library. If a series is not in the users library, it will silently be omitted from the response
parameters.name: series_id
